title: Replace raw tyre-age heuristics with a latent wear filter
summary: Recent F1 papers model tyre degradation as a latent state with pit-stop resets and show that timing data can recover hidden tyre pace better than simple surface features.
rationale: Recent F1 papers model tyre degradation as a latent state with pit-stop resets and show that timing data can recover hidden tyre pace better than simple surface features. That is a stronger formulation than fixed compound life constants or raw TyreLife alone.
implementation_hint: Within each Driver x Race stint, run a lightweight online state-space or Kalman-style filter using LapTime (s), LapTime_Delta, Cumulative_Degradation and PitStop resets. Emit filtered wear level, wear slope, one-step residual, uncertainty and a cliff/alarm indicator as tabular features for the tree model.
expected_effect: Sharper next-lap hazard near tyre cliff events and better handling of slow laps that preserve tyres, including Safety Car breathing periods.
risk: Filter parameters can overfit if estimated globally; tune only inside training folds and compare against a very simple deterministic baseline.
